texto: God lives in my soul, and I must spend my life scrubbing my soul clean of any trace of sin so that it derserves to host his presence. Repentance is a daily chore; at each morning prayer session we repent in advance for the sins we will commit that day. I look around at the others, who must sincerly believe in their inherent evil, as they are shamelessly crying and wailing to God to help them expunge the yetzer hara, or evil inclination, from their consciousness.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Deborah Feldman sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “God lives in my soul, and I must spend my life scrubbing my soul clean of any trace of sin so tha…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, crying, repentance, útil em momentos de bom-dia e termino.

texto: Can anyone survive without faith, however its labeled? No matter how you live, it seems, you need faith to get by, to get ahead.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Deborah Feldman que provoca sentido da existência e experiência vivida. Ao questionar “Can anyone survive without faith, however its labeled? No matter how you live, it seems, you need…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre fe, faith, getting-ahead-in-life, com foco em getting-ahead-in-life e survival.

texto: Later, in my adulthood, I will read the book again, even watch the movie, and understand that I wasn’t equipped, as a child, to make room for arguments that would undermine every single choice made for me, that would shatter the foundations of my very existence. I would see that I had to believe everything I was taught, if only to survive. For a long time I wouldn’t be ready to accept that my worldview could be wrong, but I do not look back with shame at my ignorance.

texto: If my mind cannot be tied down, if my dreams cannot be diminished, then no amount of restraints can really guarantee my quiet submission.

texto: I can't bear the thought of living an entire lifetime on this planet and not getting to do all the things I dream of doing, simply because they aren't allowed. I don't think it will ever be enough, this version of freedom, until it is all-inclusive. I don't think I can be happy unless I'm truly independent.

texto: I'd rather believe in reincarnation than hell. The idea of an afterlife is much so more tolerable when returning is an option.
